title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow

input_row.title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow
input_row.description: Do not allow all requests. Enable explicit authorization.
input_row.rationale: Kubelets, by default, allow all authenticated requests (even anonymous ones) without needing explicit authorization checks from the apiserver. You should restrict this behavior and only allow explicitly authorized requests.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on each node: ps -ef | grep kubelet If the --authorization-mode argument is present check that it is not set to AlwaysAllow . If it is not present check that there is a Kubelet config file specified by -- config , and that file sets authorization: mode to something other than AlwaysAllow . It is also possible to review the running configuration of a Kubelet via the /configz endpoint on the Kubelet API port (typically 10250/TCP ). Accessing these with appropriate credentials will provide details of the Kubelet's configuration.
input_row.remediation: If using a Kubelet config file, edit the file to set authorization: mode to Webhook . If using executable arguments, edit the kubelet service file /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf on each worker node and set the below parameter in KUBELET_AUTHZ_ARGS variable. --authorization-mode=Webhook Based on your system, restart the kubelet service. For example: Internal Only - General systemctl daemon-reload systemctl restart kubelet.service
input_row.default_value: By default, --authorization-mode argument is set to AlwaysAllow .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubelet_authorization_mode_not_always_allow

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get nodes -o json` to retrieve the configuration of all nodes.
- Check the `spec` section of the Kubelet configuration for the `authorizationMode` field.
- If the Kubelet is configured via command-line arguments, use the command `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o json` to find the Kubelet pod and inspect the `command` field for the presence of `--authorization-mode`.
- Validate that the `authorizationMode` is not set to `AlwaysAllow`.
- Pass criteria: The `authorizationMode` should be set to `Webhook` or another secure option, and the command should not include `--authorization-mode=AlwaysAllow`.
- Example: If the output shows `authorizationMode: Webhook`, it passes; if it shows `AlwaysAllow`, it fails.
